verses.text: On the very day Babylon laid siege to Jerusalem, God bids him record the date. A solemn note. God marks the hour His word begins to be fulfilled.
On the very day Babylon laid siege to Jerusalem, God bids him record the date. A solemn note. God marks the hour His word begins to be fulfilled.
A parable: set on the pot, fill it with choice pieces and bones, and make it boil. The city as a caldron. The proud boast of safety becomes the image of judgment.
A parable: set on the pot, fill it with choice pieces and bones, and make it boil. The city as a caldron. The proud boast of safety becomes the image of judgment.
A parable: set on the pot, fill it with choice pieces and bones, and make it boil. The city as a caldron. The proud boast of safety becomes the image of judgment.
Woe to the bloody city, the pot whose scum clings; her blood she set 'upon the top of a rock,' not covered. Guilt exposed. Unhidden bloodshed cries openly for vengeance.
Woe to the bloody city, the pot whose scum clings; her blood she set 'upon the top of a rock,' not covered. Guilt exposed. Unhidden bloodshed cries openly for vengeance.
Woe to the bloody city, the pot whose scum clings; her blood she set 'upon the top of a rock,' not covered. Guilt exposed. Unhidden bloodshed cries openly for vengeance.
He will heap the fire and set the pot empty on the coals till the scum is burned. Refining fire. Judgment will consume the corruption that defiled the city.
He will heap the fire and set the pot empty on the coals till the scum is burned. Refining fire. Judgment will consume the corruption that defiled the city.
He will heap the fire and set the pot empty on the coals till the scum is burned. Refining fire. Judgment will consume the corruption that defiled the city.
She has wearied herself with lies, and her filth shall not be purged till His fury rests; 'I will do it.' No half-measures. God's word of judgment will be fully performed.
She has wearied herself with lies, and her filth shall not be purged till His fury rests; 'I will do it.' No half-measures. God's word of judgment will be fully performed.
She has wearied herself with lies, and her filth shall not be purged till His fury rests; 'I will do it.' No half-measures. God's word of judgment will be fully performed.
God tells him 'the desire of thine eyes' — his wife — will be taken at a stroke, yet he must not mourn aloud. A costly sign. The prophet's private grief is yielded up for God's message.
God tells him 'the desire of thine eyes' — his wife — will be taken at a stroke, yet he must not mourn aloud. A costly sign. The prophet's private grief is yielded up for God's message.
God tells him 'the desire of thine eyes' — his wife — will be taken at a stroke, yet he must not mourn aloud. A costly sign. The prophet's private grief is yielded up for God's message.
He speaks in the morning; that evening his wife dies; he does as commanded, and the people ask what it means for them. Obedience in sorrow. He bears his loss in silence to teach a stunned people.
He speaks in the morning; that evening his wife dies; he does as commanded, and the people ask what it means for them. Obedience in sorrow. He bears his loss in silence to teach a stunned people.

He answers: God will profane His sanctuary, 'the desire of your eyes,' and their sons shall fall, yet they shall not mourn but pine away — 'Ezekiel is unto you a sign.' His silent ache foretells a grief too deep for tears.

He answers: God will profane His sanctuary, 'the desire of your eyes,' and their sons shall fall, yet they shall not mourn but pine away — 'Ezekiel is unto you a sign.' His silent ache foretells a grief too deep for tears.
In the day their stronghold falls, a fugitive will come, and the prophet's mouth shall be opened to speak again. Silence broken. When judgment comes, God's word resumes its full course.
